content.text: Options for capturing and restoring focus around temporary UI.
Whether capture and restore are active.
Restore focus during cleanup if capture was called and restore was not called manually.
Fallback element to focus when the captured target is unavailable.
Stores the current focus target for the provided document.
Focuses the captured or fallback target and returns whether focus moved.
The last captured focus target, or null when nothing is captured.
* Captures the current focus target and restores it later, with per-document
Latest-ref sync: stable focus callbacks read optionsRef, so it must update every render by design.
Options for capturing and restoring focus around temporary UI.
Whether capture and restore are active.
Restore focus during cleanup if capture was called and restore was not called manually.
Fallback element to focus when the captured target is unavailable.
Stores the current focus target for the provided document.
Focuses the captured or fallback target and returns whether focus moved.
The last captured focus target, or null when nothing is captured.
* Captures the current focus target and restores it later, with per-document
Latest-ref sync: stable focus callbacks read optionsRef, so it must update every render by design.
Options shared by the imperative focus-restore utilities.
Passes `preventScroll` to the focus call when restoring focus.
* Returns the current restorable focus target, ignoring body, documentElement,
* Focuses a connected target and returns whether the document's active element
Older engines / some jsdom versions reject the FocusOptions argument; fall back to plain focus().
Returns the `Window` realm for a DOM value, or null for non-DOM values.
Realm-safe HTMLElement guard for elements from iframes or other documents.
Realm-safe Node guard using a known owner window.
Returns the shadow host when a node lives in a shadow root, else null.
Returns the deepest active element, descending through open shadow roots.
Returns true when `container` contains `target` in the composed tree, across shadow boundaries.
Returns the nearest self-or-ancestor matching `selector` in the composed tree, across shadow boundaries.
* Returns the deepest composed event target, past the retargeted shadow host.
* Returns true for form-like or editable elements that can own keyboard input,
* including select and non-text input types.
* Returns true only for enabled text-editable targets that should keep typing
